overview.tagline: A family-oriented community with hometown values and Bergen County convenience
overview.description: Dumont is a friendly, unpretentious borough of about 18,000 residents in central Bergen County. Known for its tight-knit community spirit, active volunteer fire department, and family-friendly neighborhoods, Dumont offers an affordable Bergen County lifestyle with excellent access to highways and bus routes. The borough's compact layout along Washington Avenue and its active recreation programs make it a popular choice for families seeking value without sacrificing suburban quality of life.
highlights: Strong community spirit with active civic and volunteer organizations
Affordable by Bergen County standards with solid schools
Washington Avenue provides walkable access to shops and services
Active recreation department with youth and adult programs
Convenient NJ Transit bus service to NYC

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Dumont's housing stock is primarily single-family homes with some two-family properties.
realEstate.recentTrends: Dumont has experienced steady appreciation as buyers seek affordable Bergen County options. The borough attracts first-time buyers and families looking for good value. Renovations of older homes are common, and new infill construction is gradually modernizing the housing stock.
neighborhoodNotes: North Dumont near the New Milford border features larger lots and quieter streets
Washington Avenue corridor provides walkable access to shops and dining
The area near Memorial Field is popular with families for recreation access
South Dumont near Bergenfield has a more urban feel with smaller lots

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup provided twice weekly by the borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by zone — check borough website
Trash must be in sealed containers by 6:00 AM
Bulk pickup available by scheduling with DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Weekly single-stream recycling through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Paper and cardboard on alternating weeks
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or contaminated items

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water provided by Veolia Water. Sewer service through the borough and BCUA.
details: Sewer charges on property tax bills

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Building Department processes construction and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Municipal parking available near the Washington Avenue business district.
details: Free municipal lots near Washington Avenue
Overnight street parking restricted in some areas

additionalServices.title: Senior Services
additionalServices.description: The borough offers programs and services for senior residents.
details: Senior tax freeze and deduction programs

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Loud music and disturbances prohibited between 10:00 PM and 7:00 AM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Properties must be maintained to borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be mowed and maintained
Sidewalks must be cleared of snow within 24 hours
No junk vehicles or debris accumulation
Fences and exterior structures maintained in good repair

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Standard pet licensing and control ordinances.
keyRules: Annual dog license required from Borough Clerk
Dogs must be leashed when off property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats

parking.title: Parking Regulations
keyRules: No overnight street parking 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM without permit
No parking during declared snow emergencies
Alternate side parking for street cleaning
Commercial vehicles restricted in residential areas

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and inspected.
keyRules: All rental units must be registered
Certificate of Habitability required at tenant turnover
Inspections conducted before occupancy
Landlords responsible for code compliance

other.title: Garage Sales
other.summary: Yard and garage sales permitted with restrictions.
keyRules: Permit required from Borough Clerk
Limited to 3 sales per year
Hours restricted to 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM
Signs must be removed after the sale

publicSchools.name: Dumont High School
publicSchools.notes: Huskies; offers AP courses, vocational programs, and competitive athletics

privateSchools.name: St. Mary School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school affiliated with St. Mary Parish

whyMoveHere: Affordable Bergen County living for families
Strong community spirit and neighborhood feel
Active recreation programs for all ages
Good highway and bus access for commuters
Walkable Washington Avenue with shops and restaurants

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit bus to Port Authority
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit routes 166 and 167 provide direct bus service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Washington Avenue is the primary bus corridor. Some residents drive to nearby train stations for NJ Transit rail connections.

costOfLiving.summary: Dumont offers solid value for Bergen County, with homes priced below the county median and good schools.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Near or slightly above the NJ state average
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Above the national average, consistent with the northern NJ region

cons: Property taxes are relatively high for the home values
Aging housing stock in some areas
No train station in the borough
Some traffic congestion on Washington Avenue

restaurants.name: Dumont Crystal Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner 24/7 with an extensive menu.

restaurants.name: Mario's Tutto Bene
restaurants.description: Neighborhood Italian restaurant with homemade pasta, pizza, and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: Seoul Garden
restaurants.description: Korean restaurant offering authentic dishes including barbecue, bibimbap, and noodle soups.

restaurants.name: La Hacienda
restaurants.description: Mexican restaurant popular with locals for tacos, burritos, and margaritas.

parks.name: Memorial Field / Dumont Sports Complex
parks.description: Main borough athletic complex with baseball diamonds, football field, track, tennis courts, and playground.

parks.name: John F. Kennedy Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with playground, picnic areas, and open green space.

parks.name: Weiss Field
parks.description: Athletic fields used for youth soccer and recreational sports.

events.name: Dumont Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with carnival rides, food vendors, live music, and fireworks.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade honoring veterans, featuring school bands, veterans' organizations, and community groups.

events.name: Holiday Parade and Tree Lighting
events.description: Borough holiday celebration with parade, tree lighting, and community festivities.

attractions.name: Dumont Public Library
attractions.description: Community library with programs for children, teens, and adults, plus digital resources.

attractions.name: Dixon Homestead
attractions.description: Historic stone house dating to the 18th century, maintained by the Dumont Historical Society.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the first and third Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Dumont Borough Hall, 50 Washington Ave, Dumont, NJ 07628
